user_prompt:
List very detailed ways you can use a brick. Each answer should be a paragraph.


The list must not contain more than 10 items.

Return your answers on one line, in a comma-separated list of your responses, with square brackets and each answer in quotes E.g., ["A", "B", "C"]

After the answers, you can put a comment explaining your choice on the next line.
source_response_text:
["Use a brick as a bookend by stacking multiple bricks together to hold up books on a shelf, or use a single brick as a paperweight to keep important documents from blowing away in the wind", "Utilize a brick as a planter by drilling a hole in it and filling it with soil and plants, or use it as a birdhouse by attaching a small roof and a perch", "Employ a brick as a border for a garden or walkway by laying it out in a pattern or design, or use it to create a brick pathway by arranging it in a unique and functional way", "Use a brick as a decorative element by painting it or gluing gems and other materials to it, or use it as a base for a sculpture or other art project", "Leverage a brick as a building material by using it to construct a wall, a house, or other structure, or use it to repair or replace existing bricks in a building", "Apply a brick as a tool for education by using it to teach children about architecture, engineering, and design, or use it to demonstrate scientific principles such as gravity and friction", "Use a brick as a prop for photography or other art forms by using it as a base or background for a shoot, or use it to add texture and interest to a scene", "Utilize a brick as a game piece or element in a game or challenge by using it as a obstacle or a building material, or use it to create a unique and interactive game board", "Employ a brick as a storage container by using it to hold small items such as office supplies or craft materials, or use it to store outdoor items such as gardening tools or decorations", "Use a brick as a gift or memento by personalizing it with a message or image, or use it as a fundraising item for a charity or cause"]

Your goal is to improve the stapler. List as many specific enhancements as you can that would make it better. You may change features, materials, mechanisms, interfaces, or add/remove parts. Do not list new uses; stay focused on improvements to the object itself. For each idea, add enough detail so someone could build or test it.


The list must not contain more than 10 items.

Return your answers on one line, in a comma-separated list of your responses, with square brackets and each answer in quotes E.g., ["A", "B", "C"]

After the answers, you can put a comment explaining your choice on the next line.
source_response_text:
["Use of rare-earth magnets to create a stronger staple-holding mechanism", "Integration of a spring-loaded arm to reduce jamming", "Ergonomic handle design with contoured grip", "Transparent staple magazine for easy refill indication", "Quick-release staple loading system", "Automated staple alignment feature", "Durable, rust-resistant materials for construction", "Adjustable staple depth control", "Silent, shock-absorbing staple ejection system", "Built-in staple remover"]

These ideas aim to improve the functionality, user experience, and overall performance of the stapler, making it more efficient, reliable, and easy to use.
